title: ZXPong Guide
description: Complete ZXPong guide covering the release-facing match loop, controls, Solo play, World PVP, coins, achievements, and leaderboards.
summary: A compact ZXPong guide for players now that the app is live on the App Store.

sections.heading: Start here
paragraphs: ZXPong is a short-session arcade paddle game: defend your side, return the ball, and build longer rallies.
For the current release-facing build, focus on Solo play and World PVP. Local PVP is not advertised here because it is hidden by the current release flag.

sections.heading: Core guide path
paragraphs: Read How to Play for the match loop, Controls for paddle discipline, and Game Modes for release-facing mode status.
After that, use Coins, Achievements, Leaderboards, and Tips & Strategy to plan repeat sessions.
bullets: How to Play: goal, session flow, scoring, and rallies
Controls: paddle movement, positioning, and timing
Game Modes: Solo play, World PVP, and Local PVP status
Progression: BarkinMad Coins, achievements, leaderboards, and skins

sections.heading: Release check
paragraphs: This documentation is aligned to the live App Store release, with only future artwork updates expected.
Pages that lack dedicated imagery use a temporary image until replacement ZXPong-specific images are available.

images.caption: Dedicated page images will be added as artwork is released

featureShowcases.heading: ZXPong complete guide
featureShowcases.summary: The Guide connects the full ZXPong documentation set into one route through setup, play, progress, and support.
body: This matters because the ZX Series pages should feel consistent across all games. The guide helps players move from the overview into the exact page they need without losing context.
Players use this page as the main map when they want a complete explanation rather than a single topic.
bullets: Connects setup, controls, modes, tips, achievements, leaderboards, FAQ, and screenshots.
Keeps the documentation route consistent across ZXSnake, ZXBrick, ZXPong, and ZXSpace.
Uses existing artwork as the current guide placeholder.

image.caption: ZXPong hero artwork represents the full guide route.

featureShowcases.heading: From play to progress
featureShowcases.summary: ZXPong documentation explains what players do during a session and what they can review afterward.
body: This matters because arcade games are easy to start but deeper when players understand scoring, progress, and goals.
Players use the guide to connect active play with achievements, leaderboards, BarkinMad Coins, and support pages.
bullets: Supports both first-session learning and repeat play.
Keeps progression pages tied to gameplay.
Makes image presentation consistent with StudioDash and Game of Darts pages.

image.caption: ZXPong supporting artwork connects gameplay with progress surfaces.
